posts.headline: KurdistanWatch reframes the Pentagon Peshmerga funding cut as a structural pivot to Baghdad-centred channels, not an MoU technicality
posts.summary: Argues the FY27 elimination of direct Peshmerga stipend funding cannot be explained away as a lapsed memorandum and reflects a broader US policy shift toward funnelling Iraq aid through Baghdad rather than Erbil.

posts.headline: KurdistanWatch flags the KRG Peshmerga Ministry's denial as inconsistent with the Pentagon's own FY27 budget submission to Congress
posts.summary: Critiques the Ministry's public rejection of the funding cut by pointing to the explicit reductions in the official Pentagon budget documents already transmitted to Congress for 2027.

posts.headline: AMrym corroborates an undeclared Iranian green light for ships to exit the Strait of Hormuz
posts.summary: Quote-tweeting Mohammed Saleh Sedghian, AMrym says Sedghian's reporting confirms his own line that Tehran has informally cleared stranded vessels to leave Hormuz, separate from Trump's announced Project Freedom escort. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: AMrym calls Iraq's political elite disconnected from reality as cabinet horse-trading drags
posts.summary: Quote-tweeting OneNewsIQ, AMrym says senior Iraqi political figures are operating in a parallel universe relative to ground conditions, framed against the live cabinet-formation period under Zaidi. Translated from Arabic.

posts.headline: UAE leaves OAPEC after exiting OPEC+, completes the pull-out from the Arab petroleum cooperation architecture
posts.summary: Reports the UAE has formally withdrawn from the Organization of Arab Petroleum Exporting Countries, the 1968 Arab oil cooperation body, completing its exit from the regional petroleum-bloc stack on the same week it left OPEC+.

posts.headline: Iran public-sector workers unpaid for 34 days, told to wait another 22 days for salaries
posts.summary: Retweets Afshin Ismaeli's report that Iranian public-sector employees have not received pay for 34 days and have now been told they will wait another 22 days, a wartime fiscal-stress signal Bacharelhalabi is choosing to surface.
